docs.title: Third-party Modules
docs.description: None has been made yet! Are you making the first one?

docs.title: Loader
docs.description: The utilized micro on DUELink modules include a built-in loader from ST. It allows for firmware update using USB DFU class. ST provides STM32CubeProgramm to aid in loading programs, among many other things. This is a nice professional choice but it is not easy and we didn’t to leave you with that!

docs.title: Specifications
docs.description: DUELink unifies many aspects allowing modules to come in any shape and form and still conform to unified user experience.

docs.title: Arduino
docs.description: We recommend using DueDuino for a first-class experience with Arduino and DUELink.

docs.title: BeagleBoard
docs.description: BeagleBoard has many open-source offers and they all work with the DUELink ecosystem.

docs.title: micro:bit
docs.description: micro:bit includes edge pads allowing it to plug into accessories.

docs.title: Phone & Tablet
docs.description: DUELink module versatility allows it to work with Phones and Tablets. The modules on-board Scripting engine is accessible from many Interfaces and in this case a serial UART connection is all it takes. When connecting to an android system, any DUElink module will present itself as a virtual serial connection. You can quickly try it by installing any serial terminal program to send command.

docs.title: Raspberry Pi
docs.description: There are multiple ways to take advantage of the DUELink ecosystem with the entire Raspberry Pi product line.

default.title: Markdown page example
default.description: You don't need React to write simple standalone pages.
